21 And when Rehoboam was come to Jerusalem, he assembled: all the house of Judah, with the tribe of Benjamin, a hundred and four-score thousand chosen men, which were warriors, to fight against the house of Israel, to bring the kingdom again to Rehoboam the son of Solomon.

21 And when Rehoboam was come to Jerusalem, he assembled: all the house of Judah, with the tribe of Benjamin, a hundred and four-score thousand chosen men, which were warriors, to fight against the house of Israel, to bring the kingdom again to Rehoboam the son of Solomon.

21 And when Rehoboam was come to Jerusalem, he assembled: all the house of Judah, with the tribe of Benjamin, a hundred and four-score thousand chosen men, which were warriors, to fight against the house of Israel, to bring the kingdom again to Rehoboam the son of Solomon.

Our present biblical text (1 Kgs. 12:20) indicates that only the tribe of Judah followed the house of David, but the Septuagint, the Greek Old Testament, includes Benjamin in the southern kingdom (see footnote b to 1 Kgs. 12:20; cf. 2 Chron. 11:12; 15:9). The tribe of Simeon had also been assimilated into Judah by this time (see Bible Map 9), and Levites and others from the various northern…

Our present biblical text (1 Kgs. 12:20) indicates that only the tribe of Judah followed the house of David, but the Septuagint, the Greek Old Testament, includes Benjamin in the southern kingdom (see footnote b to 1 Kgs. 12:20; cf. 2 Chron. 11:12; 15:9).
